entries.statement: Skill in translating a business question into a structured analytical approach, including identifying the appropriate data sources, defining the analysis scope, and selecting the correct methodology.
Skill in querying and extracting data from relational databases, data warehouses, and APIs using SQL and programmatic methods, including writing performant queries against large datasets.
Skill in transforming and preparing data for analysis, including cleaning, normalization, format conversion, joining disparate sources, and handling missing or inconsistent values.
Skill in performing exploratory data analysis, including generating summary statistics, identifying distributions and outliers, and formulating hypotheses from initial data examination.
Skill in performing regression analysis, including model specification, assumption checking, diagnostic interpretation, and communicating results to non-technical stakeholders.
Skill in creating statistical models for business applications, including model selection, parameter estimation, validation, and performance evaluation against business criteria.
Skill in designing and executing segmentation analyses that produce actionable customer, product, or operational groupings, including cluster validation and segment profiling.
Skill in designing and analyzing controlled experiments, including hypothesis specification, randomization design, power analysis, and result interpretation with appropriate caveats.
Skill in performing time series analysis and forecasting, including trend identification, seasonality decomposition, model fitting, and communicating forecast uncertainty.
Skill in correlating and synthesizing data from multiple sources and tools to produce integrated analytical findings.
Skill in building data visualizations that accurately represent analytical findings, selecting appropriate chart types, and applying design principles that minimize misinterpretation.
Skill in building dashboard layouts that communicate KPIs clearly, prioritize decision-relevant information, and support self-service exploration by business users.
Skill in designing BI semantic models that are performant, reusable, and understandable by business users, including star schema design, measure definition, and hierarchy construction.
Skill in constructing data narratives that translate analytical findings into business context, including structuring presentations, selecting supporting evidence, and calibrating detail to audience.
Skill in producing analysis documentation that is reproducible, clearly reasoned, and accessible to both technical and non-technical reviewers.
Skill in designing analytical studies that produce defensible causal or correlational conclusions, with appropriate methodological controls and documented limitations.
Skill in designing and managing complex analytical projects, including scoping, methodology selection, stakeholder alignment, timeline management, and deliverable structuring.
Skill in performing trade-off and cost-benefit analyses that quantify competing options and present decision-relevant comparisons to stakeholders.
Skill in implementing automated data pipeline tests, including unit tests for transformations, integration tests for end-to-end flows, and regression tests for analytical outputs.
Skill in configuring and maintaining data monitoring and alerting systems, including defining thresholds, building health dashboards, and establishing escalation procedures for data quality issues.
Skill in performing data profiling and quality assessment, including completeness checks, consistency validation, and documenting data limitations that affect analytical conclusions.
Skill in conducting market research and competitive analysis using quantitative and qualitative methods, including survey design, secondary data analysis, and synthesis of findings.
Skill in mentoring junior analysts, including code and methodology review, feedback delivery, and progressive skill development aligned to analytical competency frameworks.
Skill in applying design thinking methods to analytical problem framing, including user research, requirement elicitation, iterative prototyping of analytical solutions, and stakeholder validation.
Skill in performing geospatial analysis, including spatial joins, distance calculations, area aggregations, and presenting location-based findings through maps and spatial visualizations.
